title: Monte Carlo Event Generation
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Monte Carlo Event Generation: MC event generator tuning: extract parton shower and hadronization parameters from LEP/LHC event shape distributions. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.ps.parton_shower_dipole…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=5 with effective condition number kappa_eff~1000.0; non_perturbative_power_corrections, color_reconnection_model set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.world_state_x: Pythia8/Herwig7 tune parameters (alpha_s_ISR, alpha_s_FSR, Lund_a, Lund_b, sigma_p)
E.observation_y: event shape distributions (thrust, sphericity, C-parameter) and particle multiplicities

W.regime: Existence of the recovered shower_parameter_vector is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 1000.0); non_perturbative_power_corrections dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Monte carlo statistical sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: chi2/dof ~ 1.0 for good tune; event shapes within 10%. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~1000.0. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |non_perturbative_power_corrections|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
